# Route paid orders with Shopify and Airtable on autopilot

**Pattern:** Order Fulfillment Â· **Level:** Advanced Â· **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Route paid orders with Shopify and Airtable on autopilot. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual order ops is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
On Shopify Event â†’ Stamp & Shape Order â†’ Payload Valid? â†’ Route by Work Type â†’ Process 'domestic' â†’ Airtable â†’ Merge Routed Work â†’ Tag Pipeline Metadata â†’ â€¦

## Use it for
- Teams that want to order fulfillment without manual effort
- Anyone automating order ops in e-commerce
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; Google OAuth2; Shopify access token; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: ðŸŸ¦ entry Â· ðŸŸª AI Â· ðŸŸ« actions Â· â¬œ utilities Â· ðŸŸ© outputs Â· ðŸŸ¥ error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath â€” read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger â€” hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click â†’ Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Unrecognized work types are parked in a dead-letter lane instead of erroring the whole run or silently dropping the item â€” new event kinds will show up eventually, and this is where you'd notice one without losing it. One kind is deliberately routed through a sub-workflow to show how heavier per-type logic can be extracted without touching this router at all.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered â€” push further:

1. Send a work-type value that matches no switch rule and confirm it lands in the dead-letter branch.
2. Add a new work type and its own handling branch.
3. Replace the direct-write branch with a sub-workflow call, matching the pattern already used for the first kind.

## ðŸ›Ÿ Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3Ã— with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail â†’ context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ðŸ›  Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see ðŸ”‘ lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your order ops process. Every node card explains its role â€” safe to extend at any marked branch.
